I think this is pretty close, but some cleanups are still needed.  My main concern is about the removal of the dedicated recovery thread to handle reconnection if the main ptlrpcd thread is blocked if the target is down.

Patch Set 10:

Fan Yong, if you are reworking this patch series, I'd prefer if this patch came before the statahead changes.  This is a useful change that a number of other features will also depend on (e.g. bulk IO checksum improvements), and it seems unfortunate that it might be blocked behind statahead landing if the inspections or testing for that take a long time.

Patch Set 10: Looks good to me, approved

As for the test failure seen in replay-dual.sh test_0b, I'm a bit confused whether this test is correct.  With VBR it seems possible that $MOUNT1 is recovered and replays the create of $tfile-2 before it is unmounted, regardless of whether $MOUNT2 failed to recreate $tfile.  However, you should check the kernel debug logs on the client and MDS to see if this is really what happened.  If yes, please file a separate bug to fix that test, and make sure Mike is involved.

Patch Set 10:

> Patch Set 10: Looks good to me, approved
> As for the test failure seen in replay-dual.sh test_0b, I'm a bit confused whether this test is correct. With VBR it seems possible that $MOUNT1 is recovered and replays the create of $tfile-2 before it is unmounted, regardless of whether $MOUNT2 failed to recreate $tfile. However, you should check the kernel debug logs on the client and MDS to see if this is really what happened. If yes, please file a separate bug to fix that test, and make sure Mike is involved.

After discussing with Tappro, we think the check of "checkstat $MOUNT1/$tfile-2 && return 1" is incorrect for replay-dual test_0b.

Tappro will work on such issue:
http://jira.whamcloud.com/browse/LU-661

Patch Set 13:

There is race conditions between ptlrpc_fakereq_finished() and ptlrpcd_check(), then causes the fake request cannot be released, and then causes OBD device cannot be freed. Set 13 fixed the bug.

Fan Yong, could you please also work with Liang to see if just this patch improves the SMP read or write performance. It would be interesting to try the single and multi-threaded client read IOR performance used by Ihara for the crc32c bug.

parents.subject: LU-760 obdecho: initialization and awk problem

commit.subject: ORNL-22 general ptlrpcd threads pool support
commit.message:
ORNL-22 general ptlrpcd threads pool support

Originally, there were two ptlrpcd threads on each node to serve all
async RPCs on the node, one ptlrpcd is for BRW, the other is for all
others. Such load mode cannot match more and more async RPCs process
on current large SMP node.

So we introduce ptlrpcd threads pool, any ptlrpcd threads in the pool
can be common shared by all async RPCs, like async I/O, async glimpse
lock, statahead, and ect. The async RPC sponsor can affect the system
load mode by specifying load policy when pushes the RPC into ptlrpcd
queue. On the other hand, it supports some flexible binding policies
to bind some ptlrpcd threads on CPU cores for reducing cross-CPU data
traffic, and also allow some ptlrpcd threads to be scheduled freely
on any CPU core to try to guarantee processing async RPCs in time.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <yong.fan@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: Icc0bd689df73b6863cc9adc544c3654c046cb8bd
